sourceMovie.title: Forbidden Fruits
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Forbidden Fruits works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Death Becomes Her
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Forbidden Fruits worked for you, Death Becomes Her (1992) is a strong next pick. The two films share horror and comedy throughlines and unease that lingers after the credits, even though the stories go in different directions.

faq.question: What should I watch after Forbidden Fruits?
faq.answer: Try Death Becomes Her, The Curse of Bridge Hollow, The Little Shop of Horrors—each shares DNA with Forbidden Fruits while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Forbidden Fruits?
faq.answer: Forbidden Fruits (2026) is best described as Horror, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Forbidden Fruits worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
